human_verification.notes: Reviewed the automotive-and-mobility customer-ownership-experience package for specialty boundary quality, authority-source quality, VIN and traceability evidence handling, quality-hold and supplier-boundary coverage, escalation realism, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to automotive ownership-experience work with VIN, RO, campaign, warranty, parts, supplier, lot, serial, traceability, hold, and release-aware handling.

Authority sources cover APQC and ISO complaint workflow references, AIAG APQP, PPAP, Service PPAP, and MMOG/LE standards, GS1 traceability guidance, and NHTSA recall, TSB, and EWR baselines with refresh and decay rules.

Evaluation scenarios cover policy exceptions, missing prerequisites, traceability conflicts, quality holds, supplier substitutions, handoffs, repeat failures, terminology handling, source conflicts, and tenant adaptation.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/automotive-and-mobility/customer-ownership-experience-specialist/ to keep this overlay in the canonical NAICS-derived path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around quality release, supplier approval, engineering signoff, legal interpretation, recall closure, and discretionary compensation authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated automotive ownership-experience overlay where quality, engineering, supplier, logistics, legal, pricing, and safety-release authority remain external.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is separately promoted and committed.
